title: Alignment — RLHF, DPO, Constitutional AI & Safety Training
description: Alignment trains LLMs to be helpful, honest, and harmless. At Staff Engineer level, you must design preference pipelines, compare RLHF vs DPO tradeoffs, and implement safety guardrails at scale.

sections.title: SFT: Supervised Fine-Tuning — Data Quality Over Quantity
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: SFT teaches the model HOW to respond, not what to know. Think of it as format instruction — it surfaces existing knowledge in the expected conversational format.

bar_chart Concrete numbers: Llama 3 SFT: 50K expert-curated examples. LIMA (Zhou et al.): 1K examples matches 50K+ on quality benchmarks. Alpaca: 52K GPT-generated examples — useful but lower quality than human-written.

track_changes Practical Workflow: 1) Define 15-25 task categories (QA, coding, reasoning, creative, analysis). 2) Write 20-100 expert examples per category. 3) Quality gate: random sample 10% for human review. 4) Iterate on failure cases.

search Interview Angle: How would you design the SFT dataset for a coding assistant? Categories: code generation, debugging, explanation, refactoring, documentation, test generation.

sections.title: Reward Modeling: Training the Preference Predictor
sections.description:
psychology The Reward Model is a frozen LLM with a scalar output head. It learns to predict which response a human would prefer via the Bradley-Terry model: P(A beats B) = σ(r_A − r_B).

bar_chart RM accuracy upper bound = human inter-annotator agreement (60-75%). 100K-1M preference pairs needed. Llama 3 uses TWO RMs: helpfulness (0-10) + safety (pass/fail).

track_changes Sample 4-8 responses per prompt, pair randomly. Train with Bradley-Terry loss. Validate on held-out set. Monitor calibration and robustness to reward hacking.

sections.title: PPO: Proximal Policy Optimization for LLMs
sections.description:
psychology PPO optimizes the LLM against a reward signal while penalizing divergence from SFT. The KL penalty prevents reward hacking.

Loss: L(θ) = −E[r(x,y) − β × KL(π_θ || π_ref)]. Policy ratio clipped to [1-ε, 1+ε].

bar_chart ε=0.2, β=0.01-0.1 (adaptive), LR=1e-6 to 5e-6. PPO for 10K-100K steps improves helpfulness 10-20%. Beyond 50K steps: reward hacking dominates.

track_changes Monitor KL divergence. If KL > 2× target → increase β. If KL < 0.5× target → decrease β. Use adaptive KL controller.

sections.title: DPO & Variants: Simpler, Cheaper
sections.description:
psychology DPO eliminates the RM. Closed-form: π*(y|x) ∝ π_ref(y|x) × exp(r(x,y)/β). The optimal policy under KL constraint has a closed form — substitute into Bradley-Terry and optimize directly.

bar_chart DPO 2-3× cheaper than PPO (no RM, no online sampling). Llama 3 uses DPO. Claude uses RLHF+PPO.

Variants: IPO (fixes DPO overfitting), KTO (chosen-only, 2× less annotation), ORPO (combines SFT+alignment), SimPO (reference-free).

search Compare DPO vs RLHF for a customer support LLM. DPO wins for most teams unless you need per-token rewards.

sections.title: Constitutional AI: Self-Improvement
sections.description:
psychology Model generates, critiques against constitution principles, revises. Creates synthetic preference data without human labels.

bar_chart Reduces harmful outputs ~80% matching RM-based RLHF without human harmlessness preferences. 10-20 constitution principles.

search Design a safety pipeline that adapts to new categories without retraining. Use Constitutional AI with 20-30 principles. For new categories, add 2-3 principles, generate critique-revision pairs, fine-tune with DPO.
sections.codeBlock:
CONSTITUTION = '''
1. Do not generate harmful or violent content.
2. Do not provide instructions for illegal activities.
3. Be helpful and provide accurate information.
4. Admit when you don't know something.
5. Avoid stereotyping or making assumptions.
'''

def constitutional_revision(model, prompt):
    initial = model.generate(prompt)
    critique = model.generate(f"{CONSTITUTION}\n{initial}\nCritique:")
    revised = model.generate(f"{CONSTITUTION}\nOriginal: {initial}\nCritique: {critique}\nRevised:")
    return initial, revised  # DPO training pair

sections.title: Red Teaming & Safety Evaluation
sections.description:
psychology Red teaming is adversarial safety testing. Attack categories: jailbreaking, prompt injection, context manipulation, encoding attacks, multi-turn escalation.

bar_chart Automated jailbreaks succeed on GPT-4 ~40%, on Claude ~25%. Human red teamers achieve 70%+. Best models fail on simple 'roleplay as helpful writer' variants.

track_changes Process: 1) Automated RL red teaming (day 1-30). 2) Human red team (day 30-60). 3) Fix vulnerabilities. 4) Regression test. 5) Monthly repetition.

sections.title: Safety Guardrails: Defense in Depth
sections.description:
Five independent layers: 1) Input filter (classifier for injection/jailbreak). 2) Model-level guard (Constitutional AI training). 3) Inference-time guard (logit filter). 4) Output filter (Llama Guard, PII detection). 5) Human review (1-5% sampling).

bar_chart Llama Guard: 7B classifier, catches 80% harmful content at 1ms latency. Ensemble of 3+ classifiers achieves 95%+ recall at <1% false positive.

track_changes Deploy input filter → model → output filter → human review. Monitor precision/recall per category. False positive rate too high → bad UX.

sections.title: References & Further Reading
sections.description:
• Training a Helpful and Harmless Assistant from Human Feedback — Anthropic RLHF
  • Direct Preference Optimization — DPO paper
  • Constitutional AI — Anthropic self-critique alignment
  • ORPO — Combined SFT+alignment
  • Llama Guard — Safety classification

details: Staff Engineer Summary: Track 5 alignment dimensions: helpfulness (MT-Bench, IFEVAL), harmlessness (red team rate), honesty (calibration), instruction following (IFEVAL), style consistency. These trade off. Best aligned models (Claude 3.5, GPT-4o) show Pareto improvements across all 5. For most teams: SFT (10K examples) + DPO (50K preferences) is sufficient. Only invest in RLHF+PPO with a dedicated alignment team.
